us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Witnesses say ICE officer fatally shot Houston driver through passenger window
bullets: Three detained men told a lawyer the Houston driver was shot through a passenger window.
A lawmaker said ICE confirmed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was not the operation’s target.
The shooting has intensified scrutiny of immigration enforcement tactics in Houston.

articles.title: Graham Platner formally withdraws from Maine Senate race
bullets: Platner submitted paperwork Friday to exit Maine’s U.S. Senate race.
Maine Democrats said they will hold a July 25 convention to pick a replacement nominee.
The withdrawal reshapes a closely watched race against Republican Sen. Susan Collins.

articles.title: Large heat dome forecast to bring dangerous temperatures across much of the US
bullets: Meteorologists warned of an unusually large and long-lasting heat dome.
The system is expected to affect much of the Lower 48 over the coming days.
The National Weather Service described the coming heat as significant and dangerous.

articles.title: Trump fires remaining Election Assistance Commission leaders
bullets: Trump fired the Election Assistance Commission’s only two remaining members.
The move left the agency without a quorum, according to CNN’s report.
The firings follow a Supreme Court ruling expanding presidential removal power over independent agencies.

articles.title: Trump and Iranian leader trade threats as mediators try to salvage deal
bullets: Trump and Iran’s leader escalated threats as the interim deal faltered.
Trump cited threats to assassinate him while discussing possible U.S. retaliation.
Mediators continued efforts to prevent further escalation in the Middle East conflict.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: China evacuates more than 1 million as Typhoon Bavi nears
bullets: Chinese authorities evacuated over 1 million people ahead of Typhoon Bavi.
The storm also brought strong winds and rain to Taiwan and Japan’s southern islands.
Officials reported deadly landslides in the Philippines linked to intensified monsoon rains.

articles.title: Spain battles deadly wildfire that killed at least 12
bullets: Hundreds of firefighters worked Saturday to contain the southern Spain wildfire.
Authorities said at least 12 people were killed in one of Spain’s deadliest blazes.
Aircraft and helicopters joined ground crews as heat complicated firefighting.

articles.title: Russia attacks Ukraine as Kyiv says it damaged Russian oil vessels
bullets: Russian missile and drone attacks killed two people and wounded 12, Ukrainian officials said.
Ukraine said its forces damaged 21 oil tankers and several other vessels in the Sea of Azov.
Russia disputed the scale of the Ukrainian account and reported one death from drone strikes.

articles.title: US demands Iran publicly guarantee Strait of Hormuz remains open
bullets: U.S. officials demanded Iran state that the Strait of Hormuz is open to shipping.
Washington also wants Tehran to say ships in the corridor will not be attacked.
Iranian and Omani officials were set to discuss the strait in Oman.

articles.title: Israeli strike kills Gaza World Cup screening organizer
bullets: An Israeli strike killed a Palestinian aid official who helped organize public World Cup screenings.
Local health officials said three others were also killed in the attack.
The strike came just before the Egypt-Argentina match kicked off.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Police arrest dozens after West Bengal rape-murder protests turn violent
bullets: Police said 35 people were arrested after protests over the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl.
The girl’s body was found in a pond in Baruipur, West Bengal, after she went missing.
Authorities said one innocent person was lynched during the unrest.

articles.title: US indictment links Lawrence Bishnoi gang to attacks on Indian celebrities
bullets: The U.S. Department of Justice indictment cited alleged Bishnoi gang violence against cultural figures.
The Indian Express reported the indictment referenced attacks tied to Salman Khan, Gippy Grewal and Sidhu Moose Wala.
The case forms part of a wider probe into the gang’s alleged transnational network.

articles.title: Himachal Pradesh bans mining as heavy rain batters state
bullets: The state imposed a complete mining ban through September amid monsoon risks.
Heavy rain damaged vehicles, blocked highways and submerged a bridge in Kinnaur.
The Shimla Met Office issued a yellow alert for isolated heavy rain through July 15.

articles.title: Modi and Albanese sign India-Australia defence and energy agreements
bullets: Narendra Modi and Anthony Albanese concluded the 3rd Annual Summit in Melbourne.
The leaders issued declarations on defence, maritime security and trade cooperation.
Australia and India also finalized arrangements to facilitate uranium exports to India.

articles.title: Delhi High Court questions Centre over wildlife board functioning
bullets: The court heard a PIL challenging the composition and conduct of the wildlife board’s standing committee.
Petitioners alleged the panel has rapidly approved protected-area diversion proposals.
The bench sought a government response within four weeks.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top market movers and market themes

articles.title: SK Hynix surges in Wall Street debut
bullets: SK Hynix shares soared in their U.S. trading debut.
The listing drew attention to investor demand for AI memory-chip exposure.
AP said the debut helped show Wall Street’s appetite for AI winners remains strong.

articles.title: WD-40 jumps after stronger-than-expected quarterly profit
bullets: WD-40 shares rose sharply after its latest quarterly report.
AP said the stock jumped 10.6% after profit topped analyst expectations.
The move made WD-40 one of Friday’s notable individual gainers.

articles.title: Delta stock swings after earnings beat and guidance update
bullets: Delta reported quarterly results Friday and reaffirmed its full-year profit outlook.
Reuters said the carrier’s forecast signaled confidence that fare gains can hold.
Shares drew active trading as investors weighed higher fuel costs and demand strength.

articles.title: SpaceX falls back below market debut price
bullets: Axios reported SpaceX closed at $145.30 on Friday.
The stock fell 4.5% and moved back below its $150 debut price.
The decline stood out amid a broader day of modest gains for major indexes.

articles.title: Chip stocks wobble as investors eye SK Hynix listing
bullets: Schwab noted weakness in Western Digital, Intel and Sandisk early Friday.
The moves came as investors focused on SK Hynix’s U.S. debut.
Major indexes struggled for direction while semiconductor positioning shifted.

articles.title: Delta reports $1.4 billion pre-tax profit despite record fuel expense
bullets: Delta reported June-quarter 2026 financial results on Friday.
The airline said it delivered $1.4 billion in pre-tax profit.
Management cited broad demand strength while absorbing its highest quarterly fuel expense.

articles.title: WD-40 raises outlook after strong fiscal third quarter
bullets: WD-40 reported fiscal third-quarter results for the period ended May 31.
The company highlighted sales growth and updated full-year expectations.
Its board also approved a new $100 million share repurchase program.

articles.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5 billion in record foreign IPO in US
bullets: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in its U.S. listing.
TechCrunch described it as the biggest foreign IPO in U.S. history.
The offering came amid intense investor demand for AI infrastructure suppliers.

articles.title: Disney+ weighs free streaming tier, report says
bullets: Disney+ is considering making some content available to watch for free.
The report said Disney executive Adam Smith discussed the idea at a town hall.
A free tier could help Disney compete with YouTube, Tubi and other ad-supported services.

articles.title: Netflix explores always-on live TV channels
bullets: Netflix is reportedly considering continuous live channels for subscribers.
The idea would give viewers 24/7 programming instead of only on-demand choices.
TechCrunch said the move could support Netflix’s advertising business.

articles.title: Meta removes controversial Instagram AI image feature after backlash
bullets: Meta removed a feature that let users reference public Instagram accounts in AI-generated images.
The company said the feature missed the mark after user and industry backlash.
The tool had launched earlier in the week as part of Meta’s AI image features.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
bullets: Apple filed a lawsuit Friday against OpenAI in federal court in Northern California.
The complaint alleges trade secret theft and breach of contract tied to former Apple employees.
OpenAI denied interest in other companies’ trade secrets in a public statement.

articles.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 model family
bullets: OpenAI introduced its GPT-5.6 family of models.
The launch followed scrutiny over model release processes and government review.
TechCrunch reported the models as OpenAI’s newest frontier release.

articles.title: OpenAI says GPT-5.6 remains preferred model for Microsoft Copilot 365
bullets: OpenAI said GPT-5.6 is the preferred model for Microsoft Copilot 365.
The statement came amid reports of strains in the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership.
The update underscored the continuing importance of OpenAI models to Microsoft’s AI products.

articles.title: CISA says it built incident playbook during an active cyber incident
bullets: CISA revealed it had to build its incident playbook during an ongoing incident.
The disclosure highlighted operational gaps at the U.S. cybersecurity agency.
TechCrunch reported the agency’s account as part of its latest cybersecurity coverage.
